text: Absolutely love Amazon Prime. Delivery is always on time and the return process is seamless. I've been a member for 6 years and it keeps getting better. The app is intuitive and I can track everything in real time.

Search results are completely dominated by ads now. I have to scroll through 10 sponsored items before I see anything organic. The algorithm used to surface relevant products — now it just surfaces whoever paid the most. Deeply disappointed.

Bought a laptop from a third-party seller on Amazon. It arrived with a cracked screen. Contacted the seller — no response for a week. Had to open an A-to-Z claim. Took 12 days to resolve. Amazon needs to vet their marketplace sellers better.

Generally great experience. My only gripe is the review system. It's clearly gamed. I can spot fake review clusters easily now — same date, same generic phrasing. Amazon knows this is happening. Why don't they fix it?

The returns process used to be easy. Now it depends entirely on which seller you bought from. I returned an item 3 weeks ago — still waiting for the refund. Support gave me 3 different answers. Horrible.

Mixed feelings. Delivery is excellent. But product discovery is getting worse. Too many low-quality products from unknown brands with inflated ratings. I spend more time researching products than I used to. That defeats the purpose.

Amazon Business is a game changer for our company. Bulk pricing, VAT invoices, multi-user accounts. Saves us hours every month on procurement. The approval workflow took some setup but works well once configured.

Subscribe & Save changed my delivery schedule without telling me. I only noticed when I got charged for something I didn't expect. The notification was buried in my emails. Need much clearer communication around schedule changes.

Received a counterfeit item. Packaging looked almost identical to the real brand. Only realised when I compared it side by side. Reported it — Amazon removed the listing but took no responsibility. Unacceptable for a platform this size.

Alexa integration with shopping is underrated. I can reorder household staples by voice. Works 90% of the time. The 10% failure rate is annoying but I've gotten used to it. Would rate 5 stars if voice ordering was more reliable.

Prime Video is decent but the UI is confusing. I keep getting recommended things I've already watched. The 'continue watching' list is a mess. On the shopping side — good. On the streaming side — needs work.

Customer service quality has dropped significantly. Agents seem to be following a script and have no ability to actually resolve issues. Was told to 'wait 3-5 business days' four times in a row for different problems. Escalation is almost impossible.

The Subscribe & Save feature saves me so much time for regular household items. I love that I can adjust quantities easily. Delivery is always reliable. The discounts add up over time — genuinely saves money.
Filter functionality on mobile is poor. If I set a price range, half the results are still outside that range. The 'Prime only' filter sometimes doesn't work. Basic stuff that should just work.
